question_text: A car is travelling along a straight road at a constant speed of 20 m/s. What can you conclude about the forces acting on the car?

options.label: A
options.text: The driving force is greater than the friction and air resistance

options.label: B
options.text: The forces are balanced and the resultant force is zero

options.label: C
options.text: There are no forces acting on the car

options.label: D
options.text: The driving force is less than the friction and air resistance

explanation: When an object moves at constant velocity, its speed and direction don't change. This means the acceleration is zero, so by Newton's second law, the resultant force must be zero. This only happens when all forces are balanced.
